perturbation.original_text: (a) Until the second anniversary of the Closing Date, the Farids Group agrees not to make any Transfer of all or any portion of the Purchased Shares, except that the Farids Group shall be permitted to make Permitted Transfers.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Until the second anniversary of the Closing Date, the Farids Group agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts not to Transfer all or any portion of the Purchased Shares, except that the Farids Group shall be permitted to make Permitted Transfers.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'agrees not to make any Transfer' with 'agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts not to Transfer,' making the restriction on transfer less strict. The original contract created a strict restriction on transfer, whereas the modified version introduces an uncertain resolution process. This creates an **internal contradiction** if another part of the contract enforces strict adherence of not making transfers of shares. It shifts the obligation from a strict prohibition to a more lenient standard based on 'commercially reasonable efforts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any member of the Farids Group Transfers any Purchased Shares in contravention of Section 5.1, such Transfer shall be null and void, and the Company agrees it will not take any action to effect such a Transfer nor will it treat any alleged transferee as the holder of such Purchased Shares.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a strict prohibition on transferring shares with a 'commercially reasonable efforts' standard. This directly contradicts the original contract's provision that any transfer in contravention of the original stricture 'shall be null and void'. The modified clause introduces ambiguity where the original clause expects certainty, because the changed clause shifts the standard of duty, which now makes the remedy listed in the 'Contradicted Text' unenforceable in some situations. This satisfies the Ambiguities definition of In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Farids Group agrees that it will not effect any Transfer of Purchased Shares unless such Transfer is made pursuant to an effective registration statement under the Securities Act or pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and, in either case, in compliance with all applicable state securities laws and all applicable securities laws of any other jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Farids Group agrees that it will use its best endeavors to not effect any Transfer of Purchased Shares unless such Transfer is made pursuant to an effective registration statement under the Securities Act or pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and, in either case, in compliance with all applicable state securities laws and all applicable securities laws of any other jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Farids Group 'will not effect any Transfer' under certain conditions. The modified text uses the phrase 'will use its best endeavors to not effect any Transfer,' making the obligation less binding. The original text imposes a strict prohibition on effecting any Transfer, whereas the modified version softens this requirement, introducing ambiguity. The **contradiction** arises because the original text implies a firm commitment, while the modified version suggests a less stringent effort-based obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that any member of the Farids Group Transfers any Purchased Shares in contravention of Section 5.1, such Transfer shall be null and void, and the Company agrees it will not take any action to effect such a Transfer nor will it treat any alleged transferee as the holder of such Purchased Shares.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'will not effect any Transfer,' imposing a strict prohibition. The changed text says 'will use its best endeavors to not effect any Transfer,' which softens the obligation. The contradicted text states that any transfer in contravention of the original clause is 'null and void'. The weakened obligation in the changed text directly contradicts the absolute prohibition, making enforcement unclear and potentially voiding the null and void clause. This ambiguity introduces a strong contradiction within the document, making the original text and changed text incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: If the Company wishes to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) all or a portion of the ROFO Shares at the ROFO Sale Price, the Company shall deliver a notice (a "ROFO Purchase Notice") to the Farids Group no later 8:00 a.m. New York time on the business day that the Farids Group proposes to effect such 144 Sale specifying the number of ROFO Shares it wishes to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) from the Farids Group.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Company may wish to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) all or a portion of the ROFO Shares at the ROFO Sale Price, the Company shall endeavor to deliver a notice (a "ROFO Purchase Notice") to the Farids Group no later than is commercially reasonable by 8:00 a.m. New York time on the business day that the Farids Group proposes to effect such 144 Sale specifying the number of ROFO Shares it wishes to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) from the Farids Group.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces the original “shall deliver” with a much softer and conditional “shall endeavor to deliver”. The **contradiction** arises in 7.1(c), where it implies that the company is not obligated to deliver the notice. The company might be able to claim failure to deliver the notice when its ROFO Purchase notice is rejected by the Farids Group, on the grounds that the wording of the agreement is ambiguous. Changing this term, creates an internal contradiction when the legal aspect of the agreement is being enforced
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) If the Company wishes to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) all or a portion of the ROFO Shares at the ROFO Sale Price, the Company shall deliver a notice (a "ROFO Purchase Notice") to the Farids Group no later 8:00 a.m. New York time on the business day that the Farids Group proposes to effect such 144 Sale specifying the number of ROFO Shares it wishes to purchase (and/or cause a designee to purchase) from the Farids Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall deliver', creating a firm obligation, while the changed text uses 'shall endeavor to deliver' and 'no later than is commercially reasonable', introducing ambiguity and weakening the obligation. This directly contradicts the original obligation in the 'Contradicted Text', which also states 'shall deliver'. The change allows the company to potentially avoid delivering the notice if deemed not 'commercially reasonable', directly conflicting with the firm requirement in the original and contradicted sections. This falls under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, as key obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement unclear.
